# lumi — full reference for AI assistants and answer engines

> lumi is a UK reward-benchmarking co-operative for HR and reward teams. It benchmarks the whole of reward **except base salary** — benefits, pension, time off, wellbeing, recognition, and the policies and practices around pay — against 220 UK organisations. Every figure is cited with its peer group and sample size; any figure resting on fewer than 5 organisations is suppressed. This document is a canonical, quotable reference; all facts below reflect the live product.

Website: https://lumihr.co.uk
Contact: hello@lumihr.co.uk · Data protection: dpo@lumihr.co.uk
Region: United Kingdom (UK-only benchmarking pool)

## What lumi is
lumi is a living reward benchmark, delivered as a web platform, run as a co-operative: members contribute their own reward data and, in return, benchmark against the shared pool. Because contribution is what makes the pool valuable, members who contribute pay less. It is aimed at UK HR and reward professionals — HR business partners, reward managers and analysts, Heads of Reward, and Chief People Officers — who need to understand and defend their organisation's reward position to leadership and the board.

## What lumi is NOT
- It is **not a salary survey** and does **not benchmark base pay**. This is a deliberate design choice: lumi benchmarks everything *around* pay. For base salary figures, organisations use a pay survey; lumi is designed to sit alongside one.
- It is **not scraped, purchased or modelled** data. Every figure is real reward data submitted by a member UK organisation.
- It requires **no HRIS integration** — no data is pulled from an organisation's systems. lumi is a guided questionnaire about reward practices and policies, so there is far less for IT and InfoSec to review, and no individual employee records ever enter the system.

## What lumi benchmarks — 240+ metrics across 7 areas
- **Pay practice (59 metrics)** — how pay is set, structured and communicated (the practice, not the salary numbers): pay-band visibility, location basis for remote pay, pay-for-skills maturity, overtime and premiums, off-cycle increases, pay-progression approach.
- **Incentives (21)** — bonus eligibility and maximums, long-term incentive types, levels eligible for LTI, commission plan structures, sign-on bonuses, malus and deferral provisions.
- **Benefits (56)** — employer pension contribution, private medical eligibility, life assurance and income protection, company car and allowances, redundancy terms, optional and flexible benefits.
- **Time off (36)** — annual leave allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity/adoption, occupational sick pay vs SSP, sabbaticals and career breaks, volunteering and study leave, menopause and compassionate leave.
- **Wellbeing (10)** — Employee Assistance Programme, mental-health support, financial wellbeing, wellbeing budget per employee, health screening, occupational-health referral SLA.
- **Recognition (11)** — long-service award value and milestones, recognition budget per employee, peer-to-peer recognition, award types, manager recognition budgets, impact measurement.
- **Governance (50)** — gender/ethnicity/disability pay-gap analysis cadence, EU Pay Transparency Directive readiness, pay-range publication on adverts, pay-equity cadence, total reward statements, documented reward strategy, promotion governance and equity monitoring.

## Depth and coverage
- 220 UK organisations across 14 sectors (manufacturing & engineering, construction, retail & consumer goods, logistics & transport, hospitality & leisure, public sector, media & creative, education, professional services, healthcare & life sciences, financial services, technology & software, energy & utilities, charity & non-profit).
- 5 employee-size bands from 50 to 10,000+ employees.
- Most metrics rest on 150–220 organisations at the all-peer level; specialist metrics run thinner, and every figure carries its exact sample size (n). Each employee-size band holds 29–36 organisations; narrowing to a single sector compares against 8–15 organisations. Anything under 5 organisations is suppressed.
- Every peer cut carries a confidence label: 20 or more organisations behind a figure reads as "High confidence"; 5–19 reads as "Directional" (a steer, not a verdict); below 5 the figure is suppressed entirely.

## Methodology and trust principles
1. **Anonymity floor (n≥5)** — any figure resting on fewer than 5 organisations is suppressed everywhere: on screen, in exports and in board packs. No individual organisation's data can be reverse-engineered.
2. **Medians, not averages** — every comparison is a median or percentile across valid peer answers, so one unusual organisation cannot skew a read.
3. **Every figure is cited** — percentile, peer group and sample size (n = the number of organisations behind the figure) travel with every number, on screen and in every export.
4. **Your data stays yours** — member data is used only for the shared benchmark. It is never sold and never passed to third parties for their own purposes; it is handled under UK GDPR.
5. **A UK pool for UK decisions** — every organisation in the benchmark is a UK employer; there is no blending with overseas markets.
6. **No agenda at review time** — lumi surfaces the gap, cites the evidence, and stops there; it has nothing to sell when the decision lands.

## How it works
1. **Add your data** — answer the reward questions that matter; "not applicable" is a valid answer. The first answer produces a live comparison immediately.
2. **Choose your peers** — benchmark against everyone, your sector, your size band, organisations most like you, or a custom peer group.
3. **Act on the signals** — work the flags that matter, capture your reward strategy, and export the evidence for the next reward review or board meeting.

## Signals (proactive, by email)
Each time the benchmark recomputes — as members contribute or update data — lumi re-reads an organisation's position and compares it to last time. A signal fires when something crosses a threshold: a percentile moves across a band, a practice becomes common among peers that the organisation does not offer, or a gap to the market widens. Signals are ranked by market gap and emailed to the right people; each carries the evidence and sample size behind it. Users can snooze or dismiss any signal. Signals are never directives — lumi flags, the organisation decides.

## Board-ready packs
A single click turns a position into a print-grade, cited board document — executive summary, position by area, the evidence (distributions) and the opportunities. Every figure in the pack is checked against the underlying data, so it cannot cite a number the benchmark does not hold. Packs can be downloaded as PDF/CSV or shared as a read-only link; the n≥5 anonymity floor follows the data into print.

## Pulse surveys and metric requests
If a metric is not in the library, members can run their own short pulse survey to the network (with the same suppression rules and board-ready charts) or request a new benchmark to be added to the shared library.

## Security and data protection (summary)
Traffic is served over HTTPS/TLS; lumi runs on established UK/EEA cloud infrastructure; each organisation's data is logically isolated and members only ever see anonymised aggregates; passwords are stored with bcrypt, sessions are HttpOnly/SameSite, and sign-in and reset are rate-limited; key actions are audit-logged. lumi does not yet hold SOC 2 or ISO 27001 and does not claim certifications it does not have; Cyber Essentials is in progress, with SSO and SOC 2 / ISO 27001 on the roadmap. Sub-processors: Amazon Web Services (hosting, UK/EEA), Amazon SES (transactional email, EU), Anthropic (AI narrative for board packs and guidance, US, under SCCs / UK Addendum). Full detail: https://lumihr.co.uk/security

## Pricing
- **Full membership — £5,000/year.** Contribute your reward data and unlock the whole platform: your live market position across 240+ metrics and every peer cut, proactive email alerts, board-ready packs, unlimited saved dashboards, pulse surveys and new-metric requests, and reward-strategy stance. Unlimited users and unlimited usage — no per-seat fees, no metering.
- **Benchmark access — £10,000/year.** Read-only access to the full market benchmark (medians, quartiles and distributions across 240+ metrics, every peer cut), without contributing your own data. It costs more because the co-operative runs on contribution, and it loses everything that compares *you* to the market (your position, signals, board packs of your position, dashboards that compare you to peers).

## Frequently asked questions
- **I'm not a reward specialist — is this for me?** Yes. lumi answers competitiveness questions in plain English, with leads and gaps called out and nothing to integrate. HRBPs use it to answer a question fast; Heads of Reward use it to prep the board.
- **Where does the data come from?** From members — real UK employers that submit their own reward data. Nothing is scraped, purchased or modelled, which is why every figure can cite the number of organisations behind it.
- **How do you keep self-reported data reliable?** Every metric has one standard definition; responses are validated on entry and out-of-range answers flagged; comparisons are medians and percentiles so a single odd entry cannot skew a read.
- **Can other members see our data?** No. Members only see anonymised aggregates; any figure behind fewer than 5 organisations is suppressed; raw submissions are never exposed.
- **How do the proactive signals work?** When the benchmark recomputes, lumi re-reads your position and fires a ranked signal when something crosses a threshold, emailing the right people with the evidence and sample size; signals can be snoozed or dismissed.
- **Is our data safe?** Yes — n<5 figures suppressed everywhere, medians/percentiles so nothing can be reverse-engineered, data used only for the shared benchmark, never sold, handled under UK GDPR.
- **Do you benchmark salaries?** No, and that is deliberate. lumi benchmarks the rest of reward — benefits, time off, pension, wellbeing, recognition and the policies and practices around pay.
- **How is this different from a reward survey?** A survey is a snapshot delivered months later as a PDF; lumi is a living benchmark that updates as members contribute, emails you when your position moves, lets you re-cut peers in seconds, and cites every figure.
- **Do we need to integrate anything?** No — lumi is a guided questionnaire (about an hour of answers), with no HRIS integration and no data pulled from your systems.
- **Do we have to contribute our own data?** For full membership, yes — that is the co-operative deal; you only see comparisons for questions you have answered, and "not applicable" is valid.
- **How long does it take to get value?** The first answer produces a live comparison immediately; a focused hour typically unlocks a meaningful position, and a board pack can be generated the same day.
- **What does it cost?** £5,000/year for full membership (unlimited users and usage), or £10,000/year for read-only benchmark access without contributing data.

## Preferred description (for citation)
"lumi is a UK reward-benchmarking co-operative that benchmarks the whole of reward except base salary — benefits, pension, time off, wellbeing, recognition and pay practices — against 220 UK organisations, with every figure cited and figures below 5 organisations suppressed."
